content:
# Material Design 3 - Overview

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: app navigation, mobile app shell, tablet productivity app, multi-section product
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/navigation-drawer/overview

Visual traits:
- material
- hierarchical
- surface-based
- destination-focused

Use when:
- app navigation
- mobile app shell
- tablet productivity app
- multi-section product

Avoid when:
- single-page landing page
- simple 3-tab mobile app
- dense data table itself

AI summary: Use Material navigation drawers for multi-section app shells: stable top-level destinations, clear active item, separated utility links, and careful focus/open-close behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Layout Rules

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: app navigation, mobile app shell, tablet productivity app, multi-section product
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/navigation-drawer/overview

- Use navigation drawers for top-level destinations in products with multiple sections.
- Keep labels short and destinations stable.
- Separate primary destinations from secondary settings/help items.

AI summary: Use Material navigation drawers for multi-section app shells: stable top-level destinations, clear active item, separated utility links, and careful focus/open-close behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Component Patterns

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: app navigation, mobile app shell, tablet productivity app, multi-section product
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/navigation-drawer/overview

- navigation drawer
- destination item
- active item
- divider
- scrim
- modal drawer
- standard drawer

AI summary: Use Material navigation drawers for multi-section app shells: stable top-level destinations, clear active item, separated utility links, and careful focus/open-close behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Interaction States

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: app navigation, mobile app shell, tablet productivity app, multi-section product
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/navigation-drawer/overview

- open
- closed
- active
- hover
- focus
- modal scrim

AI summary: Use Material navigation drawers for multi-section app shells: stable top-level destinations, clear active item, separated utility links, and careful focus/open-close behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Accessibility Notes

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: app navigation, mobile app shell, tablet productivity app, multi-section product
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/navigation-drawer/overview

- Active destination must be programmatically and visually clear.
- Drawer open/close should preserve focus behavior.

AI summary: Use Material navigation drawers for multi-section app shells: stable top-level destinations, clear active item, separated utility links, and careful focus/open-close behavior.

# Material Design 3 - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: app navigation, mobile app shell, tablet productivity app, multi-section product
Evidence: direct_spec / medium
Source: https://m3.material.io/components/navigation-drawer/overview

- Use for mobile/tablet apps where bottom nav is insufficient.
- Avoid using drawer as a dumping ground for unrelated actions.

AI summary: Use Material navigation drawers for multi-section app shells: stable top-level destinations, clear active item, separated utility links, and careful focus/open-close behavior.
